charts.description: Polymerase chain reaction: thermostable-polymerase amplification of a target DNA sequence through repeated denaturation, primer annealing, and extension, with a gel size check before the product is accepted.

sources.title: Primer-directed enzymatic amplification of DNA with a thermostable DNA polymerase
sources.authors: Saiki, R. K.; Gelfand, D. H.; Stoffel, S.; Scharf, S. J.; Higuchi, R.; Horn, G. T.; Mullis, K. B.; Erlich, H. A.

sources.title: Specific synthesis of DNA in vitro via a polymerase-catalyzed chain reaction
sources.authors: Mullis, K. B.; Faloona, F. A.

sources.title: Molecular Cloning: A Laboratory Manual (3rd ed.)

nodes.label: Template DNA
nodes.detail: Target DNA template containing the sequence to amplify

nodes.label: Forward + reverse primers
nodes.detail: Oligonucleotide primer pair flanking the target

nodes.label: dNTPs, Taq, Mg2+ buffer
nodes.detail: Reaction components: dNTPs, thermostable Taq polymerase, Mg2+ buffer

nodes.label: Assemble mix on ice
nodes.detail: Combine template, primers, and master mix on ice

nodes.label: Initial denaturation 95C
nodes.detail: Initial denaturation to separate template strands

nodes.label: Denature 94C
nodes.detail: Per-cycle strand denaturation

nodes.label: Anneal primers ~55C
nodes.detail: Primers anneal to complementary sequences near the target Tm

nodes.label: Extend at 72C (Taq)
nodes.detail: Taq polymerase extends primers 5'->3'

nodes.label: Cycles complete?
nodes.detail: Have all (~25-35) thermal cycles run?

nodes.label: Final extension 72C
nodes.detail: Final extension to complete partial products

nodes.label: Correct amplicon size?
nodes.detail: Agarose gel check: single band at expected size

nodes.label: Amplified target DNA
nodes.detail: Purified, correctly sized amplicon

charts.description: Sandwich enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay: a plate-bound capture antibody traps the analyte, an enzyme-conjugated detection antibody and substrate generate colour, and absorbance is read against a cutoff to call the result.

sources.title: Enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA). Quantitative assay of immunoglobulin G
The ELISA Guidebook (Methods in Molecular Biology, vol. 516)

nodes.label: Capture antibody
nodes.detail: Antibody specific to the target analyte, used to coat the plate

nodes.label: Sample antigen
nodes.detail: Specimen containing the analyte to quantify

nodes.label: Microtiter plate
nodes.detail: 96-well polystyrene plate as solid phase

nodes.label: Coat plate, 4C overnight
nodes.detail: Adsorb capture antibody to wells overnight at 4C

nodes.label: Block with BSA
nodes.detail: Block remaining binding sites to reduce background

nodes.label: Add sample; incubate
nodes.detail: Analyte binds captured antibody

nodes.label: Wash unbound
nodes.detail: Remove unbound material

nodes.label: Enzyme-conj. detection Ab
nodes.detail: Add enzyme-labeled detection antibody; incubate

nodes.label: Wash; add TMB substrate
nodes.detail: Wash, then add chromogenic substrate; enzyme develops colour

nodes.label: Stop; read OD450
nodes.detail: Stop reaction; measure absorbance at 450 nm

nodes.label: OD above cutoff?
nodes.detail: Compare signal to standard-curve cutoff

nodes.label: Analyte quantified
nodes.detail: Positive: analyte concentration read from standard curve

nodes.label: Reported negative
nodes.detail: Signal below cutoff: analyte not detected

charts.description: Western blot: SDS-PAGE separates denatured proteins by mass, proteins are electro-transferred to a membrane, and a primary/secondary antibody pair with chemiluminescent detection reveals the target band, with a transfer/probe troubleshooting loop.

sources.title: Electrophoretic transfer of proteins from polyacrylamide gels to nitrocellulose sheets: procedure and some applications
sources.authors: Towbin, H.; Staehelin, T.; Gordon, J.

sources.title: Western blotting: electrophoretic transfer of proteins from SDS-PAGE to unmodified nitrocellulose and radiographic detection with antibody and radioiodinated protein A
Western blot: technique, theory, and trouble shooting
sources.journal: North American Journal of Medical Sciences

nodes.label: Protein lysate
nodes.detail: Cell/tissue protein extract

nodes.label: Primary antibody
nodes.detail: Antibody specific to the target protein

nodes.label: SDS-PAGE gel
nodes.detail: Polyacrylamide gel for denaturing electrophoresis

nodes.label: Denature & load samples
nodes.detail: Boil in SDS/reducing buffer; load wells

nodes.label: SDS-PAGE: separate by MW
nodes.detail: Electrophorese to resolve proteins by molecular weight

nodes.label: Electroblot to membrane
nodes.detail: Transfer proteins to nitrocellulose/PVDF

nodes.label: Block membrane
nodes.detail: Block with milk/BSA to limit nonspecific binding

nodes.label: Probe primary Ab; wash
nodes.detail: Incubate primary antibody; wash

nodes.label: Secondary Ab-HRP; wash
nodes.detail: Incubate HRP-conjugated secondary antibody; wash

nodes.label: Add ECL; image
nodes.detail: Add chemiluminescent substrate; capture image

nodes.label: Band at expected MW?
nodes.detail: Is there a specific band at the target molecular weight?

nodes.label: Detected protein band
nodes.detail: Confirmed target protein signal

charts.description: Genomic DNA extraction: cells are lysed and proteins digested, then DNA is purified either by organic (phenol:chloroform) extraction or by silica-column binding, precipitated/eluted, and accepted only when the A260/A280 purity ratio is acceptable.

sources.title: Molecular Cloning: A Laboratory Manual (3rd ed.)
Rapid and simple method for purification of nucleic acids
sources.authors: Boom, R.; Sol, C. J.; Salimans, M. M.; Jansen, C. L.; Wertheim-van Dillen, P. M.; van der Noordaa, J.

sources.title: Isolation of High-Molecular-Weight DNA Using Organic Solvents

nodes.label: Cell/tissue sample
nodes.detail: Starting biological material

nodes.label: Lysis buffer + proteinase K
nodes.detail: Detergent lysis buffer with proteinase K

nodes.label: Lyse cells; digest protein
nodes.detail: Disrupt membranes and digest proteins to release DNA

nodes.label: Purification method?
nodes.detail: Choose organic extraction or silica-column purification

nodes.label: Phenol:chloroform extract
nodes.detail: Partition proteins to organic phase; recover aqueous DNA

nodes.label: Bind DNA to silica column
nodes.detail: Bind DNA to silica in chaotropic salt

nodes.label: Wash column
nodes.detail: Ethanol washes remove salts and contaminants

nodes.label: Ethanol precipitate
nodes.detail: Precipitate DNA from aqueous phase with ethanol

nodes.label: Elute / resuspend DNA
nodes.detail: Elute or resuspend purified DNA in buffer

nodes.label: A260/A280 ~ 1.8?
nodes.detail: Spectrophotometric purity check (~1.8 for DNA)

nodes.label: Purified genomic DNA
nodes.detail: Clean genomic DNA ready for downstream use

charts.description: Agarose gel electrophoresis: DNA fragments loaded alongside a size ladder migrate through an agarose matrix toward the anode and separate by size, are stained and visualised under UV, and re-run at adjusted conditions if bands are not resolved.

sources.title: Agarose gel electrophoresis for the separation of DNA fragments
sources.authors: Lee, P. Y.; Costumbrado, J.; Hsu, C. Y.; Kim, Y. H.

sources.title: Molecular Cloning: A Laboratory Manual (3rd ed.)

nodes.label: DNA samples
nodes.detail: Fragments to be sized, in loading dye

nodes.label: DNA size ladder
nodes.detail: Reference ladder of known fragment sizes

nodes.label: Agarose + TAE buffer
nodes.detail: Agarose dissolved in TAE/TBE running buffer

nodes.label: Cast gel with comb
nodes.detail: Pour molten agarose, set with comb to form wells

nodes.label: Load samples + ladder
nodes.detail: Load DNA and ladder into wells

nodes.label: Apply voltage; migrate
nodes.detail: DNA migrates toward the anode; smaller fragments move faster

nodes.label: Dye front near gel end?
nodes.detail: Has the tracking dye migrated far enough?

nodes.label: Stain; visualize on UV
nodes.detail: Stain with intercalating dye; image under UV

nodes.label: Bands resolved at size?
nodes.detail: Are fragments resolved at expected sizes vs ladder?

nodes.label: Sized DNA fragments imaged
nodes.detail: Gel image with fragments sized against the ladder

charts.description: Heat-shock transformation: plasmid DNA is mixed with chemically competent E. coli, a brief 42 C heat shock drives uptake, cells recover and express the resistance marker, and selective plating yields transformed colonies, with a retry loop when no colonies appear.

sources.title: Nonchromosomal antibiotic resistance in bacteria: genetic transformation of Escherichia coli by R-factor DNA
sources.authors: Cohen, S. N.; Chang, A. C.; Hsu, L.

sources.title: Studies on transformation of Escherichia coli with plasmids
Transformation of plasmid DNA into E. coli using the heat shock method

nodes.label: Plasmid DNA (marker)
nodes.detail: Plasmid carrying a selectable resistance marker

nodes.label: Selective agar + antibiotic
nodes.detail: Agar plates containing the selection antibiotic

nodes.label: Mix DNA + cells on ice
nodes.detail: Combine plasmid with competent cells; incubate on ice

nodes.label: Heat shock 42C, 45s
nodes.detail: Brief 42 C heat shock to promote DNA uptake

nodes.label: Return to ice
nodes.detail: Chill cells immediately after heat shock

nodes.label: Recover in SOC, 37C 1h
nodes.detail: Outgrowth in rich medium to express resistance

nodes.label: Plate on selective agar
nodes.detail: Spread cells on antibiotic plates

nodes.label: Incubate 37C overnight
nodes.detail: Grow colonies overnight

nodes.label: Colonies on plate?
